content.claim: Hungary has higher between-school variance and lower share of top performers (9.3% vs. 12.6% OECD average) and higher share of low performers (28.1% vs. 23.0%) consistent with early-tracking systems, though not proven caused solely by tracking
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Performance profiles are observational; causal attribution to tracking requires quasi-experimental or experimental design
Variance patterns could reflect multiple institutional factors beyond selection age
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
